name: Week 1 Day 4 Morning Flow
description: Hip-heavy morning flow before KB Conditioning day. Built around the @biolifex 8-exercise mobility flow for hip opening and spinal mobility, plus light core and deep hip stretches from @_fitnesshub. Everything is sore — this is about moving through it.

exercises.notes: Squat to standing, head stays down, hands touching toes. Wake up the hips.
Keep hips low, alternate sides. Opens adductors and inner hips.
Easy twist from forward fold. Spine rotation + hamstring stretch.
Step from standing to pigeon, hold briefly. Deep hip opener.
Pulse hips forward and back. This is where the magic happens for sore hips.
Breathe with movement — inhale arch, exhale round. Spine + shoulders.
Figure-four position, lean forward gently. Gets deep into the glutes.
Smooth transitions, slide forward through the floor. Feels amazing on sore everything.
Opposite arm/leg. Keep lower back glued to floor.
Extend opposite arm/leg, hold 2 seconds. Core stability without loading the spine.
Easy reps — blood flow for chest/tris recovery from push day.
Slow and controlled — also hits hip flexors which need the work today.
Each side — keep hips square, fold over front leg. Deep glute/hip stretch.
Each side — elbows down for deeper stretch. Hip flexors and groin.
Gently press knees down, hinge from hips. Inner thighs.
Keep shoulders down, controlled side-to-side. Spinal rotation + hip release.

tips: Block 1 is the whole @biolifex mobility flow — do it as one continuous sequence, no rest
Everything is sore and that's fine — move THROUGH it, not around it
Core is only 2 rounds today — just enough to wake up the stabilizers
Pushups are pure blood flow — do NOT push hard, your chest is still recovering
Block 4 deep stretches: just breathe into them and let gravity work
Total: ~30 min on a mat. You'll feel way better after this.
